description_long: This apartment building features a steeply pitched roof with two prominent chimneys and three dormer windows, suggesting an upper attic or loft space. The facade is characterized by a combination of stonework on the ground floor, forming a series of large arches, and timber-framed construction with visible wooden beams and numerous shuttered windows on the upper floors. The building's exterior displays a weathered, historic appearance, with muted blue-gray roofing and aged wooden elements. The symmetrical arrangement of windows and dormers, along with the repetitive arches, gives the structure a balanced and orderly look. The ground level appears open, possibly serving as a covered walkway or arcade, while the upper levels are enclosed residential spaces. The overall design evokes a traditional European or early American architectural style, blending stone and wood for both durability and aesthetic appeal.
description: A historic-style apartment building with a steep roof, stone arches at the base, and timber-framed upper floors featuring many shuttered windows and dormers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical facade with three large stone arches at the base, two chimneys, three dormer windows, and rows of shuttered windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steeply pitched, weathered roof and the stone end wall, with one chimney visible and no windows on this side.
description_view_2: Rear view nearly identical to the front, with three arches at the base, dormer windows, and rows of shuttered windows, but less architectural detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the steep roof, stone end wall, and a single chimney.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in horizontal rows across the upper floors, with evenly spaced dormer windows on the roof and matching shuttered windows on both the front and rear facades.
